---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 5f4906ceb7cdb6e0a77a466ca32b75c1b0352a4a
findings:
  - { id: judged-f-1, kind: judged, text: "dc-2 extends the verb's write beyond the frontmatter disposition layer into the report's rendered markdown body (rewriting the target finding's disposition-labeled bullet line), while parent dc-4 scopes disposition recording to 'the living deviation report's disposition layer — the layer deliberately outside the integrity digest' and establishes that the adjudicated report body is preserved verbatim. dc-2 itself flags this as 'a judgment call flagged for review' and argues the body has never been a digest input, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-4 for mutating adjudicated body prose that the parent's architecture treats as write-once judged output. (confidence 0.55)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-29 (2026-07-16): f-1 accepted, contract stands as written — no genuine conflict with parent dc-4. dc-4's 'layer deliberately outside the integrity digest' scopes disposition recording to the digest/integrity layer, and the report's rendered markdown body is verifiably never a digest or integrity input (no hash or digest call in internal/align is reachable from RenderMarkdown, RenderBody, or renderFindings), so dc-2 keeping the body in agreement with the frontmatter write does not touch the layer dc-4 protects. Because internal/dex/storyaxis.go renders the frontmatter-derived table and the raw body prose on the same permanent archived page, a frontmatter-only write would freeze a publicly rendered self-contradiction; the consistency write is the smaller-surface way to honor dc-4's letter, not a departure owing a supersedes or exempts edge. Decision stands; no edge (ADJ-26: no exempts edges this round).", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-f-2, kind: judged, text: "dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi disposition) for ac-3's disposition-recording, while parent dc-5's verb-economy decision ('one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... No new verb is added for ac-1') consolidated closure tooling onto verdi close. dc-1 argues in prose that dc-5's letter is scoped to ac-1's preflight only, and that reading is textually defensible, but the tension with dc-5's stated motive (one verb owning the closure ritual) is resolved by narrowing argument rather than by a declared supersedes/exempts edge — if the org reads dc-5 as a general no-new-closure-verbs rule, this is an undeclared conflict. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-29 (2026-07-16): f-2 rejected — no genuine conflict with parent dc-5. dc-5's verb-economy clause scopes its one-verb consolidation to ac-1's preflight specifically ('No new verb is added for ac-1') and never reaches ac-3's disposition-recording, which close's own scope (eligibility, freeze, rollup, archive-move) never touched even before this story; the parent feature's own stub is named disposition-verb, so the parent text mandates this verb rather than forbidding it. Reading dc-5 as a general no-new-closure-verbs rule would be the overreach dc-1 rebuts in prose, not an undeclared conflict this story must carry an edge for. Decision stands; no edge — f-2 is not a deviation, so an accepted-deviation-style disposition would misrecord the ledger (ADJ-29 rationale).", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-1, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-2, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-3, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-4, spec/closure-ergonomics#dc-5, spec/disposition-verb#dc-1, spec/disposition-verb#dc-2, spec/disposition-verb#dc-3, spec/disposition-verb#dc-4, spec/disposition-verb#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:5d4487efc30b82e12001285d7e31d441398856c420698e04045e0a71ff5365fc
integrity: sha256:1a64efc969866bd34cc16ffdb28b3764559f51d0f1ee2916633ff12fc753f135
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f-1\",\"text\":\"dc-2 extends the verb's write beyond the frontmatter disposition layer into the report's rendered markdown body (rewriting the target finding's disposition-labeled bullet line), while parent dc-4 scopes disposition recording to 'the living deviation report's disposition layer — the layer deliberately outside the integrity digest' and establishes that the adjudicated report body is preserved verbatim. dc-2 itself flags this as 'a judgment call flagged for review' and argues the body has never been a digest input, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-4 for mutating adjudicated body prose that the parent's architecture treats as write-once judged output.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"f-2\",\"text\":\"dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi disposition) for ac-3's disposition-recording, while parent dc-5's verb-economy decision ('one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... No new verb is added for ac-1') consolidated closure tooling onto verdi close. dc-1 argues in prose that dc-5's letter is scoped to ac-1's preflight only, and that reading is textually defensible, but the tension with dc-5's stated motive (one verb owning the closure ritual) is resolved by narrowing argument rather than by a declared supersedes/exempts edge — if the org reads dc-5 as a general no-new-closure-verbs rule, this is an undeclared conflict.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/disposition-verb@5f4906ceb7cdb6e0a77a466ca32b75c1b0352a4a], digest: sha256:5d4487efc30b82e12001285d7e31d441398856c420698e04045e0a71ff5365fc, integrity: sha256:1a64efc969866bd34cc16ffdb28b3764559f51d0f1ee2916633ff12fc753f135 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-f-1** [no-conflict]: dc-2 extends the verb's write beyond the frontmatter disposition layer into the report's rendered markdown body (rewriting the target finding's disposition-labeled bullet line), while parent dc-4 scopes disposition recording to 'the living deviation report's disposition layer — the layer deliberately outside the integrity digest' and establishes that the adjudicated report body is preserved verbatim. dc-2 itself flags this as 'a judgment call flagged for review' and argues the body has never been a digest input, but no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-4 for mutating adjudicated body prose that the parent's architecture treats as write-once judged output. (confidence 0.55) — Controller adjudication ADJ-29 (2026-07-16): f-1 accepted, contract stands as written — no genuine conflict with parent dc-4. dc-4's 'layer deliberately outside the integrity digest' scopes disposition recording to the digest/integrity layer, and the report's rendered markdown body is verifiably never a digest or integrity input (no hash or digest call in internal/align is reachable from RenderMarkdown, RenderBody, or renderFindings), so dc-2 keeping the body in agreement with the frontmatter write does not touch the layer dc-4 protects. Because internal/dex/storyaxis.go renders the frontmatter-derived table and the raw body prose on the same permanent archived page, a frontmatter-only write would freeze a publicly rendered self-contradiction; the consistency write is the smaller-surface way to honor dc-4's letter, not a departure owing a supersedes or exempts edge. Decision stands; no edge (ADJ-26: no exempts edges this round).
- **judged-f-2** [no-conflict]: dc-1 adds a new top-level verb (verdi disposition) for ac-3's disposition-recording, while parent dc-5's verb-economy decision ('one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal... No new verb is added for ac-1') consolidated closure tooling onto verdi close. dc-1 argues in prose that dc-5's letter is scoped to ac-1's preflight only, and that reading is textually defensible, but the tension with dc-5's stated motive (one verb owning the closure ritual) is resolved by narrowing argument rather than by a declared supersedes/exempts edge — if the org reads dc-5 as a general no-new-closure-verbs rule, this is an undeclared conflict. (confidence 0.30) — Controller adjudication ADJ-29 (2026-07-16): f-2 rejected — no genuine conflict with parent dc-5. dc-5's verb-economy clause scopes its one-verb consolidation to ac-1's preflight specifically ('No new verb is added for ac-1') and never reaches ac-3's disposition-recording, which close's own scope (eligibility, freeze, rollup, archive-move) never touched even before this story; the parent feature's own stub is named disposition-verb, so the parent text mandates this verb rather than forbidding it. Reading dc-5 as a general no-new-closure-verbs rule would be the overreach dc-1 rebuts in prose, not an undeclared conflict this story must carry an edge for. Decision stands; no edge — f-2 is not a deviation, so an accepted-deviation-style disposition would misrecord the ledger (ADJ-29 rationale).
